captured.evidence: central compartment — vessel-rich group including brain, heart, kidneys, liver, ~75% of CO, ~10% body mass
wakes in 8 minutes due to redistribution from brain to muscle/fat; hepatic metabolism too slow
CSHT defined as time for plasma concentration to fall 50% after stopping infusion of a given duration
peripheral compartments saturate, eliminating redistribution gradient; recovery depends on slower hepatic metabolism
fentanyl undergoes 65-75% first-pass pulmonary uptake, protecting brain from spikes
72-year-old has smaller effective central compartment, higher peaks, requires dose reduction

summary: Complete and accurate explanation of the three-compartment model, with correct mechanism for bolus redistribution versus prolonged-infusion CSHT prolongation. The student also correctly applied pulmonary first-pass uptake and elderly dosing. Nothing to fix here.
voss_quip: Textbook recall, mechanism intact, no dose errors. Now do it again at 3 a.m. with a hypotensive patient.
